info.name: Attendance Constraint Management APIs
info.description:
APIs for managing attendance constraint basic info, employees, additional locations, day shifts, shift assignment, and constraint rules.

Base URL: {{base_url}}/api/v1/attendance/constraints

All endpoints require Bearer token authentication.

request.description:
Update only the basic information of a constraint: constraint_name, constraint_type, and branch_ids. All fields are optional (send only what you want to update).

**Fields:**
- `constraint_name` (string, optional) — New name for the constraint
- `constraint_type` (string, optional) — Must be one of the valid types (e.g. `regular`)
- `branch_ids` (array of UUIDs, optional) — Array of management hierarchy IDs for branches

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
    "data": {
        "id": "constraint-uuid",
        "constraint_name": "Eastern Region Constraint",
        "constraint_type": "منتظم",
        "constraint_code": "regular",
        "branch_locations": [],
        "notes": null,
        "is_active": 1,
        "priority": 1,
        "start_date": null,
        "end_date": null,
        "max_over_time": null,
        "config": {},
        "branches": [
            {"id": "branch-uuid-1", "name": "Branch 1"}
        ],
        "created_by": "Admin User",
        "created_at": "2026-05-14 10:00:00"
    },
    "message": "Constraint basic info updated successfully"
}

request.description:
Get all employees assigned to this attendance constraint.

Returns employees from two sources:
- **main**: Employees whose `user_professional_datas.attendance_constraint_id` matches this constraint
- **additional**: Employees linked via the `attendance_constraint_user` pivot table

Each employee entry includes a `source` field indicating where the assignment comes from.

Assign an employee to this attendance constraint as their **main** constraint.

This sets the `attendance_constraint_id` column on the employee's `user_professional_datas` record.

**Note:** This is the primary constraint assignment, NOT an additional location assignment.

**Fields:**
- `user_id` (UUID, required) — The user ID to assign

Create one or more additional locations for a constraint.

These locations are stored in the `attendance_constraint_locations` table and will appear in the `additional_locations` array of the `user-constraint/today` API response for any employee assigned to this constraint as an additional constraint.

**Fields (per location):**
- `name` (string, optional) — Display name for the location
- `latitude` (numeric, required) — Between -90 and 90
- `longitude` (numeric, required) — Between -180 and 180
- `radius` (integer, required) — Geofence radius in meters (1-10000)

Get all additional locations defined for a specific constraint.

Returns locations from the `attendance_constraint_locations` table.

Update a specific additional location by its ID.

All fields are optional — send only the fields you want to update.

**Fields:**
- `name` (string, optional) — Updated display name
- `latitude` (numeric, optional) — Between -90 and 90
- `longitude` (numeric, optional) — Between -180 and 180
- `radius` (integer, optional) — Geofence radius in meters (1-10000)

Delete a specific additional location by its ID.

The location is permanently removed from the constraint.

Returns the current shift configuration in the exact same shape expected by `POST /{constraint}/shifts`, so the frontend can display and edit it without any extra mapping.

**Mode detection logic:**
- `weekly` — all enabled days share identical periods
- `daily`  — at least two enabled days have different periods

**Weekly mode response includes:**
- `mode: "weekly"`
- `days` — array of enabled day names
- `periods` — the shared period array

**Daily mode response includes:**
- `mode: "daily"`
- `schedule` — object keyed by day name, each with its own `periods`

**Always present:**
- `raw_schedule` — full 7-day map (enabled/disabled, periods, lateness_rules, early_clock_in_rules)
- `max_over_time` — current max overtime setting

response.name: Weekly mode example
Daily mode example

request.description:
Assign the same shift periods to all checked days.

Checked days (`days` array) become enabled work days with the provided periods.
All other days of the week are set to `enabled: false` (weekly holiday).

Default rules applied to every enabled day when no prior rules exist:
- `lateness_rules`: 30 minutes grace period
- `early_clock_in_rules`: 30 minutes allowed before shift start

Existing `lateness_rules` / `early_clock_in_rules` already set on each day are **preserved**.
Only `time_rules.weekly_schedule` is modified — `default_location`, `type_attendance`, and other constraint_config keys are untouched.

**Fields:**
- `mode` (string, required): `weekly`
- `days` (array of strings, required): enabled weekdays
- `periods` (array, required, min 1): shift periods shared by all checked days
  - `start_time` (HH:MM, required)
  - `end_time` (HH:MM, required)
  - `extends_to_next_day` (boolean, default false)

response.name: Success

request.description:
Assign custom shift periods per day.

Each key in `schedule` is a day name. Days present in `schedule` with at least one period are set to `enabled: true`. Days absent from `schedule` are set to `enabled: false`.

**Fields:**
- `mode` (string, required): `daily`
- `schedule` (object, required): map of day name → `{ periods: [...] }`
  - `periods.*.start_time` (HH:MM)
  - `periods.*.end_time` (HH:MM)
  - `periods.*.extends_to_next_day` (boolean)

item.name: Update Constraint Rules (lateness + early clock-in + max overtime)

request.description:
Update constraint-level rules applied uniformly to every day in the weekly schedule.

All fields are optional — send only what you want to update.

**Fields:**
- `lateness_minutes` (integer | null, 0–480): Grace period in minutes before an employee is marked late. Applied to every day. `null` clears the rule.
- `early_clock_in_minutes` (integer | null, 0–480): How many minutes before shift start an employee is allowed to clock in. `null` clears the rule.
- `max_over_time` (integer | null, min 0): Maximum overtime hours allowed. Stored on the constraint row (not per day).

**What changes:**
- `lateness_rules` on every day → `{ lateness_period: N, lateness_unit: "minute" }`
- `early_clock_in_rules` on every day → `{ allowed_minutes_before: N }`
- `attendance_constraints.max_over_time` column

Get all day shifts (weekly schedule) for a specific constraint.

Returns the full weekly schedule with periods, lateness rules, and early clock-in rules for each day of the week (Saturday through Friday).

response.name: Success
